messages.content: Malignant tumors are currently among the primary causes of death worldwide. This seriously endangers human life and health as well as restricts social and economic development. However, achieving highly sensitive and reliable detection at an early stage remains one of the major obstacles. Herein, an enhanced multi-phonon resonance Raman scattering (EMRRS) method was introduced for the early detection of gliomas with high sensitivity. EMRRS was obtained by enhancing the MRRS of ZnS through in-situ growth on Ti3C2Tx MXene. The enhancement factor reaches 1.5×107. ZnS/Ti3C2Tx MXene was biofunctionalized to prepare bioprobes for detecting the glioma marker called glial fibrillary acidic protein (GFAP). The EMRRS-based method achieved a highly sensitive detection limit as low as 104.4 ag mL-1 with a linear range of 10 ng mL-1 to 1 fg mL-1. To further improve the reliability of the detection, the photoluminescence signal provided by ZnS served to validate the EMRRS. The analysis of serum samples showed the potential of our method for clinical application. This work provides an effective way to enhance the multi-phonon resonant Raman scattering of semiconductors and demonstrates the potential of EMRRS for clinical application.

In summary, this study presents the EMRRS method for the early detection of gliomas, a type of malignant tumor, with high sensitivity. The method involves using ZnS/Ti3C2Tx MXene bioprobes to detect the glioma marker GFAP. The EMRRS method achieved a highly sensitive detection limit and showed potential for clinical application. This work also provides a way to enhance multi-phonon resonant Raman scattering of semiconductors. Overall, the study contributes to the field of tumor detection and has implications for improving human health and development.

Malignant tumors are currently among the primary causes of death worldwide. This seriously endangers human life and health as well as restricts social and economic development. However, achieving highly sensitive and reliable detection at an early stage remains one of the major obstacles. Herein, an enhanced multi-phonon resonance Raman scattering (EMRRS) method was introduced for the early detection of gliomas with high sensitivity. EMRRS was obtained by enhancing the MRRS of ZnS through in-situ growth on Ti3C2Tx MXene. The enhancement factor reaches 1.5×107. ZnS/Ti3C2Tx MXene was biofunctionalized to prepare bioprobes for detecting the glioma marker called glial fibrillary acidic protein (GFAP). The EMRRS-based method achieved a highly sensitive detection limit as low as 104.4 ag mL-1 with a linear range of 10 ng mL-1 to 1 fg mL-1. To further improve the reliability of the detection, the photoluminescence signal provided by ZnS served to validate the EMRRS. The analysis of serum samples showed the potential of our method for clinical application. This work provides an effective way to enhance the multi-phonon resonant Raman scattering of semiconductors and demonstrates the potential of EMRRS for clinical application.

Malignant tumors are a major global cause of death, posing significant threats to human life, health, and socio-economic progress. However, detecting tumors at an early stage with high sensitivity remains challenging. In this study, researchers introduced an enhanced multi-phonon resonance Raman scattering (EMRRS) method to detect gliomas, a type of malignant brain tumor, at an early stage with high sensitivity. EMRRS was achieved by enhancing the multi-phonon resonance Raman scattering (MRRS) of ZnS through in-situ growth on Ti3C2Tx MXene. The enhancement factor reached an impressive value of 1.5×107. ZnS/Ti3C2Tx MXene was then biofunctionalized to prepare bioprobes capable of detecting the glioma marker GFAP (glial fibrillary acidic protein). The EMRRS-based method demonstrated a highly sensitive detection limit of 104.4 ag mL-1, within a linear range of 10 ng mL-1 to 1 fg mL-1. To further ensure reliable detection, the photoluminescence signal provided by ZnS was utilized to validate the EMRRS results. Analysis of serum samples highlighted the potential of this method for clinical application. This study not only presents an effective approach to enhancing the multi-phonon resonant Raman scattering of semiconductors but also demonstrates the promising clinical applications of EMRRS.

ZnS/Ti3C2Tx MXene was biofunctionalized to prepare bioprobes for detecting the glioma marker called glial fibrillary acidic protein (GFAP). The EMRRS-based method achieved a highly sensitive detection limit as low as 104.4 ag mL-1 with a linear range of 10 ng mL-1 to 1 fg mL-1. Malignant tumors are a leading cause of death worldwide and pose significant threats to human life, health, and socio-economic development. However, early detection of tumors with high sensitivity remains a major challenge. In this study, researchers introduced an innovative method called enhanced multi-phonon resonance Raman scattering (EMRRS) for the early detection of gliomas, a type of malignant brain tumor, with high sensitivity. EMRRS was achieved by enhancing the multi-phonon resonance Raman scattering (MRRS) of a semiconductor material called ZnS through in-situ growth on Ti3C2Tx MXene. The enhancement factor achieved a remarkable value of 1.5×107. The ZnS/Ti3C2Tx MXene material was then functionalized with bioprobes to specifically detect a glioma marker named glial fibrillary acidic protein (GFAP). The EMRRS-based method displayed excellent sensitivity with a detection limit as low as 104.4 ag mL-1 and a linear detection range from 10 ng mL-1 to 1 fg mL-1. To further enhance the reliability of the detection, the photoluminescence signal emitted by ZnS was used to validate the EMRRS results. The investigation of serum samples confirmed the potential of this method for clinical application. This study not only provides an effective approach for enhancing multi-phonon resonant Raman scattering of semiconductors but also demonstrates the promising clinical applications of EMRRS in detecting gliomas at an early stage.

The highly sensitive and accurate bioprobes based on ZnS/Ti3C2Tx MXene composites were successfully developed for the detection of GFAP. Two-dimensional material of MXene was used for the first time to enhance the MRRS signal of semiconductors. The constructed EMRRS system has an enhancement factor of 1.5×107 and a low detection limit of 104.4 ag mL-1 for GFAP detection. A combination of EMRRS and PL signals improves the reliability of the assay. This enhanced multi-phonon resonance Raman scattering technique holds great promise for the early and timely diagnosis of tumors.

The researchers successfully developed highly sensitive and accurate bioprobes using ZnS/Ti3C2Tx MXene composites for detecting the glioma marker GFAP. MXene, a two-dimensional material, was utilized for the first time to enhance the multi-phonon resonance Raman scattering (MRRS) signal of semiconductors. The resulting EMRRS system demonstrated an impressive enhancement factor of 1.5×107 and achieved a remarkably low detection limit of 104.4 ag mL-1 for GFAP detection. To enhance the reliability of the assay, the combination of EMRRS and photoluminescence (PL) signals was employed. This enhanced multi-phonon resonance Raman scattering technique shows significant promise for the early and timely diagnosis of tumors.

The researchers successfully developed highly sensitive and accurate bioprobes using ZnS/Ti3C2Tx MXene composites for detecting the glioma marker GFAP. MXene, a two-dimensional material, was utilized for the first time to enhance the multi-phonon resonance Raman scattering (MRRS) signal of semiconductors. The resulting EMRRS system demonstrated an impressive enhancement factor of 1.5×107 and achieved a remarkably low detection limit of 104.4 ag mL-1 for GFAP detection. To enhance the reliability of the assay, the combination of EMRRS and photoluminescence (PL) signals was employed. This enhanced multi-phonon resonance Raman scattering technique shows significant promise for the early and timely diagnosis of tumors

Overall, the researchers were able to successfully create bioprobes using ZnS/Ti3C2Tx MXene composites for the specific detection of the glioma marker GFAP. The use of MXene, a two-dimensional material, allowed for the enhancement of the multi-phonon resonance Raman scattering (MRRS) signal of semiconductors, which had not been done before. The resulting EMRRS system displayed an impressive enhancement factor of 1.5×107 and achieved an extremely low detection limit of 104.4 ag mL-1 for GFAP. To improve the reliability of the assay, the researchers combined EMRRS with photoluminescence (PL) signals. This novel technique holds significant potential for the early and accurate diagnosis of tumors.
In this study, an enhanced multi-phonon resonance Raman scattering (EMRRS) method was introduced to achieve a highly sensitive detection of early glioma

tumors. The researchers enhanced the multi-phonon resonance Raman scattering (MRRS) of ZnS by growing it in-situ on Ti3C2Tx MXene. This resulted in an enhancement factor of 1.5×107. The ZnS/Ti3C2Tx MXene was then biofunctionalized to prepare bioprobes capable of detecting the glioma marker GFAP.

The EMRRS-based method achieved a highly sensitive detection limit of 104.4 ag mL-1, with a linear range of 10 ng mL-1 to 1 fg mL-1. To further enhance the reliability of the detection, the researchers used the photoluminescence signal provided by ZnS to validate the EMRRS results. The analysis of serum samples demonstrated the potential of this method for clinical application.

Overall, this study presents a highly sensitive method for the early detection of gliomas using EMRRS. The use of ZnS/Ti3C2Tx MXene composites and the validation of results with photoluminescence signals contribute to the reliability of the detection. This research has important implications for improving the early diagnosis and treatment of gliomas.

However, detecting tumors at an early stage with high sensitivity remains challenging. The enhancement factor reached an impressive value of 1.5×107. ZnS/Ti3C2Tx MXene was then biofunctionalized to prepare bioprobes capable of detecting the glioma marker GFAP (glial fibrillary acidic protein). The EMRRS-based method demonstrated a highly sensitive detection limit of 104.4 ag mL-1, within a linear range of 10 ng mL-1 to 1 fg mL-1. To further ensure reliable detection, the photoluminescence signal provided by ZnS was utilized to validate the EMRRS results. Analysis of serum samples highlighted the potential of this method for clinical application. This study not only presents an effective approach to enhancing the multi-phonon resonant Raman scattering of semiconductors but also demonstrates the promising clinical applications of EMRRS.

The early detection of malignant tumors is crucial for improving patient outcomes. In this study, the researchers introduced an enhanced multi-phonon resonance Raman scattering (EMRRS) method to achieve highly sensitive detection of early gliomas. They accomplished this by enhancing the multi-phonon resonance Raman scattering (MRRS) of ZnS through in-situ growth on Ti3C2Tx MXene. The resulting enhancement factor of 1.5×107 was impressive.

To detect gliomas, the ZnS/Ti3C2Tx MXene was biofunctionalized to create bioprobes capable of detecting the glioma marker GFAP. The EMRRS-based method exhibited a remarkable level of sensitivity, with a detection limit of 104.4 ag mL-1 and a linear range of 10 ng mL-1 to 1 fg mL-1. To further enhance reliability, the photoluminescence signal emitted by ZnS was utilized to validate the EMRRS results.

The potential of the EMRRS method for clinical application was demonstrated through the analysis of serum samples. This study not only provides an effective approach to enhance the multi-phonon resonant Raman scattering of semiconductors but also highlights the promising clinical applications of EMRRS in the early detection of gliomas.

The successfully developed highly sensitive and accurate bioprobes using ZnS/Ti3C2Tx MXene composites for detecting the glioma biomarker GFAP. The multi-phonon resonance Raman scattering (MRRS) signal of semiconductor has been enhanced for the first time by using the two-dimensional material MXene. The resulting EMRRS system demonstrated an impressive enhancement factor of 1.5×107 and achieved a remarkably low detection limit of 104.4 ag mL-1 for GFAP detection. In order to improve the reliability of detection, PL signal and EMRRS signal are introduced to verify each other. This enhanced multi-phonon resonance Raman scattering technique shows significant promise for the early and timely diagnosis of tumors.

In this study, the researchers successfully developed highly sensitive and accurate bioprobes using ZnS/Ti3C2Tx MXene composites for the specific detection of the glioma biomarker GFAP. It is noteworthy that this is the first time that the multi-phonon resonance Raman scattering (MRRS) signal of a semiconductor material has been enhanced using the two-dimensional material MXene. The result was an EMRRS system with an impressive enhancement factor of 1.5×107 and a remarkably low detection limit of 104.4 ag mL-1 for GFAP detection. In order to enhance the reliability of the detection, the researchers introduced both the photoluminescence (PL) signal and the EMRRS signal to verify each other.

This enhanced multi-phonon resonance Raman scattering technique holds significant promise for the early and timely diagnosis of tumors. The combination of ZnS/Ti3C2Tx MXene composites and EMRRS provides a highly sensitive and accurate method for detecting glioma biomarkers. The validation through PL signals further improves the reliability of the detection. This research has important implications for advancing the early diagnosis and treatment of tumors.

The multi-phonon resonance Raman scattering (MRRS) signal of semiconductor has been enhanced for the first time by using the two-dimensional material MXene. The resulting EMRRS system demonstrated an impressive enhancement factor of 1.5×107 and achieved a remarkably low detection limit of 104.4 ag mL-1 for GFAP detection. In order to improve the reliability of detection, PL signal and EMRRS signal are introduced to verify each other. This enhanced multi-phonon resonance Raman scattering technique shows significant promise for the early and timely diagnosis of tumors.

In this study, the researchers successfully developed highly sensitive and accurate bioprobes using ZnS/Ti3C2Tx MXene composites for the specific detection of the glioma biomarker GFAP. This study marks the first time that the multi-phonon resonance Raman scattering (MRRS) signal of a semiconductor has been enhanced using the two-dimensional material MXene. The resulting EMRRS system achieved an impressive enhancement factor of 1.5×107 and demonstrated a remarkably low detection limit of 104.4 ag mL-1 for GFAP detection. To enhance the reliability of the detection, the researchers introduced both the photoluminescence (PL) signal and the EMRRS signal to validate each other's results. This enhanced multi-phonon resonance Raman scattering technique shows great potential for the early and timely diagnosis of tumors. The combination of ZnS/Ti3C2Tx MXene composites and EMRRS provides a sensitive and accurate method for detecting glioma biomarkers. The validation through PL signals further improves the reliability of the detection. The findings of this research have significant implications for advancing the early diagnosis and treatment of tumors.

The multi-phonon resonance Raman scattering (MRRS) signal of semiconductor has been enhanced for the first time by using the two-dimensional material MXene. The resulting EMRRS system demonstrated an impressive enhancement factor of 1.5×107 and achieved a remarkably low detection limit of 104.4 ag mL-1 for GFAP detection. Furthermore, the PL signal of ZnS is introduced for mutual verification with EMRRS, which improves the reliability of detection. This enhanced multi-phonon resonance Raman scattering technique shows significant promise for the early and timely diagnosis of tumors.
